<python_code>
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# First, open the gripper to prepare for grasping
open_gripper()

# Get the grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Approach and grasp the handle with a z_approach to ensure safe descent
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Final grasp pose without approach

# Close the gripper to grasp the nut
close_gripper()

# Now, get the pose of the white hollow center of the brown square nut to compute the relative transform
# This will help us align the nut correctly when inserting onto the block
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Compute the relative rigid transform from the handle to the center (in object frame)
# This transform will be used to determine how the nut is oriented relative to the handle
relative_position_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position

# Convert quaternions to rotation objects
rot_handle = R.from_quat(np.roll(handle_quaternion_wxyz, -1))  # Convert WXYZ -> XYZW
rot_center = R.from_quat(np.roll(center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Relative rotation from handle frame to center frame
relative_rotation_handle_to_center = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()

# Now, get the target insertion peg pose — the white hollow center of the brown square block
block_center_position, block_center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square block')

# To insert the nut, we want the nut's center to align with the block's center
# So we compute the desired handle position and orientation based on the relative transform

# Desired center pose of the nut at the block
desired_nut_center_position = block_center_position
desired_nut_center_rotation = R.from_quat(np.roll(block_center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Now compute where the handle should be given this desired nut center pose
# Apply inverse of the relative transform
desired_handle_position = desired_nut_center_position - relative_rotation_handle_to_center.apply(relative_position_handle_to_center)

# The desired handle orientation: rotate the original handle orientation by the same delta as center alignment
# We want: handle_rot_desired = desired_nut_center_rotation * relative_rotation_handle_to_center * handle_rot_original_in_nut_frame
# But since handle_rot_original_in_nut_frame = identity in object frame logic, simplify:
desired_handle_rotation = desired_nut_center_rotation * relative_rotation_handle_to_center
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = R.as_quat(desired_handle_rotation).roll(1)  # Convert back to WXYZ

# Move above the insertion point with z_approach
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)

# Perform the final insertion motion
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Optionally, slightly retract after insertion
# goto_pose(desired_handle_position + np.array([0, 0, 0.1]), desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Open gripper to release the nut
open_gripper()

# Return to home joint configuration
goto_home_joint_position()
</python_code>

import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# First, open the gripper to prepare for grasping
open_gripper()

# Get the grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Approach and grasp the handle with a z_approach to ensure safe descent
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Final grasp pose without approach

# Close the gripper to grasp the nut
close_gripper()

# Now, get the pose of the white hollow center of the brown square nut to compute the relative transform
# This will help us align the nut correctly when inserting onto the block
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Compute the relative rigid transform from the handle to the center (in object frame)
# This transform will be used to determine how the nut is oriented relative to the handle
relative_position_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position

# Convert quaternions to rotation objects (scipy uses XYZW, so we convert WXYZ -> XYZW)
rot_handle = R.from_quat(np.roll(handle_quaternion_wxyz, -1))  # WXYZ -> XYZW
rot_center = R.from_quat(np.roll(center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Relative rotation from handle frame to center frame
relative_rotation_handle_to_center = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()

# Now, get the target insertion peg pose — the white hollow center of the brown square block
block_center_position, block_center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square block')

# To insert the nut, we want the nut's center to align with the block's center
# So we compute the desired handle position and orientation based on the relative transform

# Desired center pose of the nut at the block
desired_nut_center_position = block_center_position
desired_nut_center_rotation = R.from_quat(np.roll(block_center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Now compute where the handle should be given this desired nut center pose
# Apply inverse of the relative transform
desired_handle_position = desired_nut_center_position - relative_rotation_handle_to_center.apply(relative_position_handle_to_center)

# The desired handle orientation: rotate the original handle orientation by the same delta as center alignment
desired_handle_rotation = desired_nut_center_rotation * relative_rotation_handle_to_center
# Convert back to WXYZ for goto_pose
desired_handle_quaternion_xyzw = R.as_quat(desired_handle_rotation)
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = np.roll(desired_handle_quaternion_xyzw, 1)  # XYZW -> WXYZ

# Move above the insertion point with z_approach
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)

# Perform the final insertion motion
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Open gripper to release the nut
open_gripper()

# Return to home joint configuration
goto_home_joint_position()

<python_code>
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# Open gripper to prepare for grasping
open_gripper()

# Get the grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Approach and grasp the handle with z_approach for safe descent
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Close the gripper to grasp the nut
close_gripper()

# Get the center of the brown square nut to compute relative transform
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Compute relative position and orientation from handle to nut center
relative_position_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position
rot_handle = R.from_quat(np.roll(handle_quaternion_wxyz, -1))  # WXYZ -> XYZW
rot_center = R.from_quat(np.roll(center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))
relative_rotation_handle_to_center = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()

# Get the target block's center (insertion peg)
block_center_position, block_center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square block')

# Desired nut center pose at the block
desired_nut_center_position = block_center_position
desired_nut_center_rotation = R.from_quat(np.roll(block_center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Compute desired handle pose using inverse of relative transform
desired_handle_position = desired_nut_center_position - relative_rotation_handle_to_center.apply(relative_position_handle_to_center)
desired_handle_rotation = desired_nut_center_rotation * relative_rotation_handle_to_center
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = np.roll(R.as_quat(desired_handle_rotation), 1)  # XYZW -> WXYZ

# Approach insertion point from above
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)

# Insert the nut onto the block
goto_pose(desired_handle_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Optional: apply small upward lift after insertion to avoid friction
lifted_position = desired_handle_position + np.array([0, 0, 0.05])
goto_pose(lifted_position, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)

# Open gripper to release
open_gripper()

# Return to home
goto_home_joint_position()
</python_code>
